ogTitle: Mabins Well vs Macksville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mabins Well and Macksville.
heading: Mabins Well vs Macksville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mabins Well, New South Wales and Macksville, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mabins Well has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Macksville (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mabins Well or Macksville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mabins Well has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Macksville scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mabins Well scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Macksville scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mabins Well or Macksville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mabins Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
